name: David Njoku
headline: Herbert upgrade can't mask a two-year usage slide
body:
Njoku's profile has eroded fast. After a TE6 peak in 2023 (123 targets, 882 yards, 20.6% target share), his role thinned to 15.3% on 97 targets across 11 games in 2024, then cratered to a 9.2% share, 48 targets and just 293 yards in an 11-game, injury-shortened 2025 that left him at TE39. The efficiency softened too: 0.98 yards per route run and a 47.9% success rate are well off his 1.61 and 40.7% breakout marks, and his negative EPA per play has persisted all three seasons.

The move to Los Angeles pairs him with Justin Herbert, a clear quarterback upgrade, but he must beat out a productive young tight end for the starting job and fight a deeper target tree. The red-zone history (nine red-zone looks, four scores in 2025) keeps his touchdown floor alive, yet entering an age-30 season on a one-year deal, he's a wait-and-see streamer until the snaps confirm a real role.
